metadata.title: Implement Session Telemetry and Data Persistence

story.iWant: comprehensive session and drill data persisted to Dexie
story.soThat: future epics (Progress Tracking, Adaptive Intelligence) have rich data to analyze
story.tasks: - Create telemetry service (src/services/telemetry/logger.ts) with structured logging functions - Enhance TrainingSession to use db.transaction() for atomic multi-table session writes - Implement drillTypes distribution calculation in session completion - Add telemetry logging for session lifecycle events (session_start, drill_complete, session_end) - Create data export utility (src/services/storage/exportData.ts) with exportSessionData(sessionId) function - Implement database maintenance utility cleanOldSessions() to auto-delete sessions older than 365 days - Add comprehensive error handling with localStorage fallback for all Dexie operations - Enhance session persistence to include full drillTypes breakdown { number_line: N, spatial_rotation: N, math_operations: N } - Update TrainingSession to call telemetry logger for all session lifecycle events - Add toast notifications for Dexie write failures using sonner library - Test IndexedDB quota handling and fallback behavior

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** confidence prompts are implemented (Story 3.6 complete) **When** a user completes a training session **Then** all data is successfully persisted to Dexie:
**Sessions Table:** ```typescript await db.sessions.add({ id: auto-increment, timestamp: sessionStartTime (ISO 8601), module: 'training', completionStatus: 'completed', drillCount: 12, accuracy: 85, // percentage (0-100) confidenceBefore: 2, // 1-5 scale confidenceAfter: 4, // 1-5 scale confidenceChange: 2, // delta (after - before) duration: 647000, // milliseconds sessionType: 'quick' | 'full', drillQueue: ['number_line', 'spatial_rotation', ...] // array of drill types }); ```
**Drill Results Table:** Each drill result saved individually by drill components (already implemented in Stories 3.2-3.4): ```typescript await db.drill_results.add({ id: auto-increment, sessionId: currentSessionId, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), module: 'number_line' | 'spatial_rotation' | 'math_operations', difficulty: 'easy' | 'medium' | 'hard', isCorrect: boolean, timeToAnswer: number (ms), accuracy: number (0-100), userAnswer: number | null, correctAnswer: number, // Drill-specific fields... }); ```
**Telemetry Logs Table:** Session lifecycle events logged by telemetry service: ```typescript await db.telemetry_logs.bulkAdd([ { id: auto-increment, timestamp: sessionStart (ISO 8601), event: 'session_start', module: 'training', data: { sessionType: 'quick', drillCount: 6 }, userId: 'local_user' }, { id: auto-increment, timestamp: drillComplete (ISO 8601), event: 'drill_complete', module: 'number_line', data: { accuracy: 95.7, difficulty: 'medium', isCorrect: true }, userId: 'local_user' }, { id: auto-increment, timestamp: sessionEnd (ISO 8601), event: 'session_end', module: 'training', data: { accuracy: 85, duration: 647000, confidenceChange: 2 }, userId: 'local_user' } ]); ```
**And** Error handling: - Wrap all Dexie operations in try-catch - If IndexedDB write fails: Log to console, show user toast notification (using sonner) - Fallback: Store session data in localStorage as backup (retrieve on next launch)
**And** Data export utility (`src/services/storage/exportData.ts`): - Function: `exportSessionData(sessionId: number): Promise` - Returns JSON object bundling session + all drill results - For debugging and user data portability
**And** Database maintenance: - Auto-delete sessions older than 365 days (configurable) - Function: `cleanOldSessions(): Promise` runs on app launch - Returns count of deleted sessions - Keeps database size manageable (<10MB typical)

doc.title: Epic 3: Training & Drill Engine Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines Session, DrillResult, and TelemetryLog interfaces. Session includes id, timestamp, module, duration, completionStatus, confidenceBefore/After/Change, drillCount, accuracy, sessionType, drillQueue. DrillResult includes sessionId FK, module-specific fields. TelemetryLog structured with id, timestamp, event, module, data JSON, userId.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Comprehensive telemetry specification with event types: session_start, drill_complete, session_pause, session_resume, session_end. Each event captures specific data: session_start logs sessionType/drillCount, drill_complete logs accuracy/timeToAnswer/difficulty/isCorrect, session_end logs accuracy/duration/confidenceChange/drillCount. All entries include id, timestamp (ISO 8601), event, module, data JSON.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Session persistence patterns: Dexie write failures wrapped in try-catch, localStorage fallback for session_backup, toast notification for user ("Session data not saved"). Atomic session writes using db.transaction() for multi-table operations. Data integrity validation: check required fields (sessionId, module, isCorrect) before persistence. Auto-delete sessions older than 365 days via cleanOldSessions() on app launch to prevent quota exhaustion (<10MB typical DB size).

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: DiscalculasDB extends Dexie with 8 tables: sessions (++id, timestamp, module, [timestamp+module]), drill_results (++id, sessionId, timestamp, module), telemetry_logs (++id, timestamp, event, [timestamp+event]). LocalStorage keys: STORAGE_KEYS.STREAK, LAST_SESSION_DATE, USER_SETTINGS, SESSION_BACKUP.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Capture session start/end, step durations, dwell time, accuracy, streaks, self-reported confidence/anxiety deltas; persist locally and render in Progress dashboard. Provide CSV/JSON exports for journaling and snapshots. No cloud sync - all data stored in-browser IndexedDB + localStorage. Privacy: no PII collected, userId always "local_user".

artifact.reason: Dexie database instance with all 8 tables defined. Provides db.sessions, db.drill_results, db.telemetry_logs tables. Includes health check utility ensureDBHealth() and performance monitoring wrapper queryWithTiming(). Use this as base for all telemetry persistence operations.

TypeScript interfaces for database schemas. Session includes training-specific fields (sessionType, drillQueue, confidenceBefore/After/Change, drillCount, accuracy). DrillResult has module-specific optional fields (targetNumber, operation, problem, shapeType). TelemetryLog structure: id, timestamp, event, module, data (Record), userId.

Main session orchestrator. Already persists session to Dexie on start (lines 134-154) and updates on completion (lines 216-249). Has localStorage fallback pattern (lines 147-154, 239-249). Needs enhancement: add telemetry logging, use db.transaction() for atomic writes, calculate drillTypes distribution on completion.

React Context managing in-memory session state. Provides sessionId, drillQueue, results array, confidenceBefore/After/Change. Exposes startTrainingSession, recordDrillResult, endSession, setConfidenceBefore/After methods. Session telemetry service will use this state to log events.

Reference drill showing DrillResult persistence pattern. Uses db.drill_results.add() in try-catch block with localStorage fallback (lines 92-109). Pattern to replicate in telemetry service for consistent error handling.
Another drill persistence reference. Shows db.drill_results.add() with try-catch and localStorage fallback (lines 88-97). Demonstrates DRILL_RESULTS_BACKUP localStorage key convention.

LocalStorage utility with namespaced keys (STORAGE_KEYS.STREAK, LAST_SESSION_DATE, USER_SETTINGS, etc.). Demonstrates type-safe localStorage access pattern with validation. Use STORAGE_KEYS for session backup keys to maintain consistency.

Demonstrates localStorage-based service pattern with error handling (lines 18-26, 37-74). Includes try-catch wrapping with console.error logging and fallback return values. Good reference for telemetry service error handling approach.

node.react: UI framework with hooks and concurrent rendering
node.dexie: IndexedDB wrapper for persisting sessions, drill results, telemetry logs. Provides db.transaction() for atomic multi-table writes, bulkAdd() for batch inserts.
node.date-fns: Date utilities for timestamp formatting, session duration calculations, old session cleanup (differenceInDays, subDays)
node.sonner: Toast notification library for displaying Dexie write failure messages to users
node.fake-indexeddb: IndexedDB mock for Vitest unit tests, simulate Dexie operations and quota errors

story-context.constraints: - MUST create telemetry service at src/services/telemetry/logger.ts with functions: logSessionStart, logDrillComplete, logSessionEnd - MUST create data export utility at src/services/storage/exportData.ts with exportSessionData(sessionId) function - MUST implement cleanOldSessions() function (can be in db.ts or separate maintenance.ts file) - Telemetry logs MUST include userId: 'local_user' for all entries (privacy requirement) - Session writes MUST use db.transaction() for atomic operations across multiple tables - Bulk inserts MUST use bulkAdd() for performance (NOT individual add() calls in loop) - All Dexie operations MUST be wrapped in try-catch with console.error and toast notification on failure - localStorage fallback keys MUST use STORAGE_KEYS namespace pattern (e.g., 'discalculas:sessionBackup') - cleanOldSessions() MUST be called on app initialization (src/App.tsx or TrainingSession useEffect) - Session data MUST include drillTypes distribution: { number_line: number, spatial_rotation: number, math_operations: number } - Telemetry event timestamps MUST be ISO 8601 format (new Date().toISOString()) - exportSessionData MUST return JSON object with session + all related drill_results (JOIN operation) - Database size MUST be kept under 10MB via automatic cleanup (365-day retention configurable) - Toast notifications for errors MUST use sonner library (already in dependencies) - Error messages MUST be user-friendly ("Session data not saved" not "Dexie transaction failed")

interface.name: Session
interface.signature: interface Session { id?: number; // Auto-increment PK timestamp: string; // ISO 8601 session start (indexed) module: string; // "training" duration: number; // Milliseconds completionStatus: "completed" | "abandoned" | "paused"; sessionType?: 'quick' | 'full'; // Training session type drillQueue?: string[]; // Array of drill type strings confidenceBefore?: number; // 1-5 scale confidenceAfter?: number; // 1-5 scale confidenceChange?: number; // Delta (after - before) drillCount?: number; // Total drills completed accuracy?: number; // Accuracy percentage (0-100) }

interface.name: DrillResult
TelemetryLog
interface.signature: interface TelemetryLog { id?: number; // Auto-increment PK timestamp: string; // ISO 8601 (indexed) event: string; // 'session_start' | 'drill_complete' | 'session_end' | 'session_pause' | 'session_resume' module: string; // 'training' or specific drill type data: Record; // JSON payload with event-specific metrics userId: string; // Always 'local_user' }

interface.name: Telemetry Service
Data Export Utility
Database Maintenance

tests.standards:
Use Vitest with React Testing Library for unit/integration tests. Use Playwright for E2E tests. Test files colocated with source (.test.ts/.test.tsx).
**Dexie Testing:** - Use fake-indexeddb for mocking IndexedDB in Vitest - Test atomic transactions with db.transaction() - Test bulkAdd() operations - Simulate quota exceeded errors to test fallback behavior
**Telemetry Testing:** - Mock Dexie db.telemetry_logs.add() calls - Verify correct event, module, data payload structure - Test error handling and localStorage fallback - Verify userId always set to 'local_user'
**Export Testing:** - Test exportSessionData() joins session + drill_results + telemetry_logs correctly - Test with session ID that doesn't exist (should return null or empty) - Verify JSON structure matches interface
**Maintenance Testing:** - Test cleanOldSessions() deletes only sessions older than retention period - Test with various dates (today, yesterday, 364 days ago, 365+ days ago) - Verify returns correct count of deleted sessions - Test with empty database (should return 0)
**Error Handling Testing:** - Simulate Dexie write failures (throw error in mock) - Verify console.error called with descriptive message - Verify toast notification displayed (mock sonner toast) - Verify localStorage fallback data structure
tests.locations: - Telemetry service tests: src/services/telemetry/logger.test.ts - Export utility tests: src/services/storage/exportData.test.ts - Maintenance tests: src/services/storage/maintenance.test.ts (or db.test.ts if added there) - Integration tests: src/features/training/components/TrainingSession.test.tsx (enhance existing) - E2E tests: tests/e2e/training-session-telemetry.spec.ts
tests.ideas:
**Telemetry Service Tests:** - AC: logSessionStart logs event to telemetry_logs table → Test: Call logSessionStart('sess-123', 'quick', 6), verify db.telemetry_logs.add called with { event: 'session_start', module: 'training', data: { sessionType: 'quick', drillCount: 6 }, userId: 'local_user' } - AC: logDrillComplete logs drill metrics → Test: Call logDrillComplete('number_line', { accuracy: 95.7, difficulty: 'medium', isCorrect: true, timeToAnswer: 3247 }), verify telemetry entry created - AC: logSessionEnd logs session summary → Test: Call logSessionEnd({ accuracy: 85, duration: 647000, confidenceChange: 2, drillCount: 12 }), verify telemetry entry - AC: Error handling with localStorage fallback → Test: Mock db.telemetry_logs.add to throw error, verify localStorage.setItem called with backup key, console.error logged, no exception thrown - AC: Toast notification on Dexie failure → Test: Mock Dexie failure, verify sonner toast() called with user-friendly message
**Export Utility Tests:** - AC: exportSessionData returns session + all drill results → Test: Create session with ID=1, add 3 drill results with sessionId=1, call exportSessionData(1), verify returned object has session and drillResults array length 3 - AC: Export includes telemetry logs → Test: Add telemetry logs for session, verify exportSessionData includes them - AC: Export with non-existent sessionId → Test: Call exportSessionData(9999), verify returns null or throws error with clear message - AC: Export JSON structure matches interfaces → Test: Validate returned object against Session/DrillResult/TelemetryLog TypeScript interfaces
**Database Maintenance Tests:** - AC: cleanOldSessions deletes sessions older than 365 days → Test: Create sessions dated 366 days ago, 365 days ago, 364 days ago, call cleanOldSessions(365), verify only 366-day-old session deleted, returns 1 - AC: Default retention 365 days → Test: Call cleanOldSessions() without argument, verify uses 365 days as default - AC: Returns count of deleted sessions → Test: Create 5 old sessions, verify cleanOldSessions() returns 5 - AC: Preserves recent sessions → Test: Create session from today, call cleanOldSessions(), verify session still exists - AC: Empty database handling → Test: Call cleanOldSessions() on empty DB, verify returns 0, no errors
**TrainingSession Integration Tests:** - AC: Session completion persists with drillTypes distribution → Test: Complete session with 2 number_line + 2 spatial_rotation + 2 math_operations drills, verify session.drillQueue persisted correctly - AC: db.transaction() used for atomic writes → Test: Spy on db.transaction, verify called when session completes - AC: Telemetry logged at session lifecycle events → Test: Start session, complete drill, end session, verify 3 telemetry entries created (session_start, drill_complete, session_end)
**E2E Tests:** - AC: Complete session persists all data → Test: Navigate to /training, complete full session, open DevTools IndexedDB inspector, verify session + drill_results + telemetry_logs tables populated - AC: Data survives page refresh → Test: Complete half session, refresh page, verify data still in IndexedDB - AC: Export session data → Test: Complete session, call exportSessionData() from console, verify JSON structure correct - AC: Old sessions auto-cleaned on app launch → Test: Seed database with old sessions, reload app, verify cleanOldSessions() called and old data removed
